alternate_names: Vocabulary for Event Recording and Incident Sharing
notes: VERIS (Vocabulary for Event Recording and Incident Sharing) is the structured classification system behind Verizon's annual Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR). Uses the A4 model: Actor (who), Action (what), Asset (which), Attribute (how/CIA). Developed by Verizon RISK Team. Open source at github.com/vz-risk/veris. Version 1.3.7 is current. Community database (VCDB) tracks public incidents.

match_nodes.description: Vocabulary for Event Recording and Incident Sharing

data.official_name: Vocabulary for Event Recording and Incident Sharing
data.description: Comprehensive structured vocabulary for classifying security incidents across four dimensions (A4): Actor, Action, Asset, Attribute.
data.notes: VERIS uses dot notation for categories (e.g., action.hacking, actor.external). Powers the annual DBIR report — industry's most cited breach report. Enables statistical analysis of breach trends. JSON schema for structured data.

data.note: No per-category URL. VERIS is a vocabulary/schema for incident classification. Categories are documented in the GitHub repository and schema files.

examples.note: Action category: Unauthorized access attempts (exploitation, brute force, SQLi)
Action category: Malicious software (ransomware, backdoors, keyloggers)
Action category: Social engineering (phishing, pretexting, bribery)
Actor category: Outside the organization (criminals, nation-states, hacktivists)
Actor category: Inside the organization (malicious insiders, negligent users)
Asset category: Server infrastructure (web, database, file, mail servers)
